clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Select appropriate intravenous antihypertensive drug based on comorbidity in hypertensive emergencies
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Hypertensive emergency with SBP ≥180 or DBP ≥120 with acute target organ damage
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Comorbidity-specific drug recommendation with BP targets and warnings

navigationPath.purpose: Show preferred drug(s) for selected comorbidity
Show BP target and timing requirements
Route to ICU monitoring or step therapy

comorbidity.validation: Must be one of 8 specified comorbidities

validationRules: SBP ≥180 or DBP ≥120 for emergency classification
Comorbidity must match one of 8 categories

rules: Apply BP target based on comorbidity (especially aortic dissection: SBP ≤120 mmHg within 20 min)
Check absolute contraindications (beta blockers in pulmonary edema, ACE/ARB in eclampsia)
Flag drug interactions (PDE-5 inhibitors + nitrates = profound hypotension)
Apply beta blocker contraindications: bradycardia <60, SBP <100, 2nd/3rd degree heart block, reactive airway disease, moderate-severe LV failure with pulmonary edema
Route to appropriate monitoring setting (ICU vs step-down)

comorbidities.name: Acute aortic dissection
comorbidities.specialInstructions: Beta blockade should precede vasodilator (nicardipine/nitroprusside) to prevent reflex tachycardia

comorbidities.name: Acute coronary syndromes
contraindications: Beta blockers if: bradycardia <60, SBP <100, 2nd/3rd degree heart block, reactive airway disease, moderate-severe LV failure with pulmonary edema
Nitrates with PDE-5 inhibitors → profound hypotension

comorbidities.name: Perioperative hypertension
comorbidities.criteria: BP >160/90 mm Hg or SBP elevation ≥20% of preoperative value persisting >15 min
comorbidities.specialInstructions: Intraoperative hypertension most frequent during anesthesia induction and airway manipulation

comorbidities.name: Acute sympathetic discharge or catecholamine excess states

betaBlockerContraindicated.description: Flag if patient has beta blocker contraindications

symptomsToAsk: Acute target organ damage: chest pain (aortic dissection/ACS), dyspnea (pulmonary edema), neurologic deficits (stroke/ICH), visual changes (eclampsia), oliguria (AKI)

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Admit to ICU, initiate IV antihypertensive immediately
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Recheck diagnosis - may be hypertensive urgency, not emergency

appDisplay.screenType: Red alert crisis screen with comorbidity selector
appDisplay.behavior: Do not proceed to outpatient routing - hospital admission required

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Immediate IV antihypertensive administration in monitored setting (ICU/ED)
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Continuous BP monitoring - reassess q5-15min until stable
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: BP checked continuously via arterial line preferred
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Page on-call hospitalist/critical care physician immediately
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log comorbidity, drug administered, BP response, time to target
